data.intro: Promote diversity and organise events for new LGBT+ students
data.long_intro: As president of Grenoble Institute of Technology's LGBT+ student association, my team and I published posters promoting diversity and organised events and safe spaces for new students.

data.intro: Built a dynamic visualisation for Invo's blog and a prototype to compare Massachusetts' hospitals
data.long_intro: During this internship, I worked on rewriting the algorithms and design of a D3 data visualisation, as well as created the prototype of a web application to visualise and compare care among hospitals in Massachusetts.

data.intro: Lead and coordinate the development of high-quality Next.js (React.js) applications
data.long_intro: I've worked at Vizzuality as a front-end developer for 8 years before transitioning to Technical lead in 2022. Every year I've contributed to the development of multiple climate-focused platforms featuring complex data visualisations and geospatial analysis using Mapbox GL JS, deck.gl, D3 and visx.
technologies: TypeScript, Next.js, React.js, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui (Radix UI)
accomplishments: Led the engineering of two multi-year projects for the World Bank and an EU-funded consortium
Introduced per-branch database deployment and E2E accessibility testing pipelines to strengthen quality control across platforms
Sourced and negotiated a third-party vendor deal to automate internationalisation workflows
Delivered technical documentation, training videos and full-day workshops for clients including Mongabay and South Sudan's Ministry of Water Resources and Irrigation
Led recruitment, onboarding and mentoring of 3 junior developers

data.intro: Contribute to monthly and yearly events about cycling
data.long_intro: I regularly volunteer at monthly critical mass events to ensure safety during the rides and the annual Cykelkarnevalen where I manage the children's activities booth.

data.intro: In charge of attending people and registering their loans
data.long_intro: I volunteer up to 5 hours a week at Leksaksbiblioteket, Stockholm's first toy library, to assist with daily operations such as welcoming visitors and registering loans.
